title: Born On This Soil

mood.label: Uneasy calm 🌥️
mood.summary: A landmark ruling and record markets propped the day up while a rising quake toll, a spreading Ebola outbreak, xenophobic displacement and a dangerous heat dome held it just below neutral — an uneasy calm, neither rising nor sinking.

style.genre: dub-techno / motorik house-lite — a sampled xylophone tracing a circling topline over a steady, driving TR-707 four-on-the-floor pulse with deep tape-echo, a sampled celesta answering high in the gaps, a Rhodes-ish electric-piano pad laying ambiguous chords, a plucked upright bass walking under a soft sine sub; C# melodic minor's raised sixth and seventh lifting the minor third just enough to keep the calm honest and unsettled — a landmark held up against a heavy ledger, neither rising nor sinking

sampleSources: Strudel default library — VCSL xylophone, celesta & strings, Rhodes-ish electric piano, RolandTR707
pattern: stack( s("bd bd bd bd").bank("RolandTR707").gain(0.6).lpf(2400), s("~ ~ cp ~ ~ ~ cp ~").bank("RolandTR707").gain(0.34).room(0.34).delay(0.26).delaytime(0.375), s("~ rim ~ rim ~ rim ~ [rim rim]").bank("RolandTR707").gain(0.26).delay(0.2).delaytime(0.33).pan(0.42), s("hh*16").bank("RolandTR707").gain(sine.range(0.07,0.2).slow(8)).pan(0.6), note("<[cs3,e3,gs3] [a2,cs3,e3] [fs2,as2,cs3] [gs2,c3,ds3]>").s("gm_epiano1").attack(0.03).release(1.6).gain(0.3).lpf(sine.range(900,2100).slow(12)).room(0.4), note("cs5 ~ ds5 e5 ~ gs4 as4 ~ ~ b4 cs5 ~ e5 ~ ds5 ~").s("xylophone").gain(0.4).room(0.32).delay(0.3).delaytime(0.375).off(0.125, x => x.gain(0.15).pan(0.72)), note("~ ~ gs6 ~ ~ cs6 ~ e6 ~ ~ ~ as6 ~ ~ b5 ~").s("celesta").gain(0.22).room(0.48).rsize(3).pan(0.3).hpf(600), note("cs2 ~ cs2 gs2 ~ fs2 ~ e2").s("gm_epiano1").gain(0.32).lpf(700), note("<cs1 a1 fs1 gs1>").s("sine").gain(0.3).lpf(210), note("<cs3 a3 fs3 gs3>").s("sawtooth").attack(2).release(3).gain(0.12).lpf(600).room(0.6) )

lines: Six to three, and the old page held —
born on this soil is still a name;
the Dow rang up its second bell,
and Wall Street closed on a quarter's flame.
The strait swings open, the tankers pass,
but Doha's table stays cold as glass.
and forty thousand names still lost;
a fourth province learns the fever's creep,
and a made-up deadline counts its cost.
The heat dome sits on two hundred million heads —
we clean five hundred million off the debts.
It's an uneasy calm — don't call it fine,
one hand's a landmark, one hand's a grave;
the ledger runs in a doubled line,
some kept afloat and some not saved.
Hold the ruling and the ruin in the very same day —
born on this soil, and the toll to pay.
